title: Turnkey Growing Commercial Cleaning Franchise w Recurring Revenue
description:
This offering represents the opportunity to acquire a well-established commercial cleaning franchise serving a strong regional market. The business operates within the recession-resistant B2B facility services sector, providing recurring janitorial and specialty cleaning services to offices, medical facilities, factories, educational institutions, and other commercial clients.



With an established 9+ year tenure, the company benefits from a contract-based revenue model with repeat customers, predictable cash flow, and strong client retention. Operations are supported by an operations manager, office manager, trained staff, defined systems, and the backing of a recognized national brand that provides marketing resources, operational guidance, local coaching, and proven processes.



Current owner invests approximately 10-15 hours per week in the business. Tasks include high-level client communications/relations, ops manager and office manager oversight, financial oversight, and overall strategy. Owner is not in the day-to-day operational, hiring, or training grind. The business is positioned for continued growth through expanded sales, marketing, and prospecting efforts.



The existing business includes healthy recurring revenue. Very little overhead, with current location lease on a month-to-month agreement for <$400/month, no inventory to manage and carry, and no heavy asset burden. While the current owner operates from a leased micro-office, many franchises within this system successfully operate as home-based. New owner can manage and run from anywhere within driving distance of their clients, or further depending on buyer’s vision for scaling/expanding the business further.



Franchisor is supportive, collaborative, and foundationally set for continued growth. After full alignment and approval from the current owner, and after a signed LOI, buyer prospect will be connected with the franchisor for final approval and review of the FDD. At closing, buyer must s
